title_en: Mexico is placing an enormous bet on rail
title_zh: 墨西哥正在押注一场关于铁路的巨大赌局
rubric_en: Can it keep its plans on track?
rubric_zh: 它能让计划继续“按轨道”前进吗？

paragraphs.en: GIVEN THAT its passengers are mostly heading into work, the mood aboard El Insurgente, a train linking Mexico City with nearby Toluca, is unusually upbeat. Since it began running in February, Mexicans have enjoyed a smooth ride in sleek trains across one of the world’s most clogged and sprawling urban areas. It’s “wonderful” to avoid traffic, says one customer. On a bad day the journey between Toluca and the Mexican capital, barely 60km (40 miles) apart, can take several hours by road. El Insurgente takes about an hour and costs under $6.
paragraphs.zh: 鉴于乘客大多是上班族，连接墨西哥城和附近托卢卡的“恩苏金根特号”列车车厢里，气氛反而格外轻松。该线自今年2月开通以来，墨西哥人就能乘坐流线型列车，在世界上最拥堵、最蔓延的城市区域之一中享受平稳舒适的出行。正如一位乘客所说，“能避开堵车，太好了。” 在最坏的情况下，托卢卡和墨西哥首都（相距仅60公里，即40英里）之间的路面交通可能需要几个小时；而恩苏金根特号约需1小时，票价不足6美元。

paragraphs.en: Claudia Sheinbaum, Mexico’s president, wants more of this. She has ambitious plans to build more than 3,000km of new passenger rail before the end of her term in 2030. Many countries, such as China and Turkey, are making big bets on trains. But Mexico’s dreams are “dramatic”, says Andrew Young, a rail analyst. Mexico is starting more or less from scratch, in a region not known for trains, and building with speed, he says.
paragraphs.zh: 墨西哥总统克劳迪娅·谢因鲍姆希望更多人都能体验到这种便利。她计划在2023年至2030年任期结束前，新建3,000公里以上的客运铁路。像中国和土耳其这样的国家都在大举押注铁路。铁路分析师安德鲁·杨说，墨西哥的蓝图是“很激进”的。因为墨西哥基本是从零开始，在一个并不以铁路见长的地区推进，且建设节奏很快。

paragraphs.en: Mexico once had a passable passenger network. By the 1990s, however, the rail system was deteriorating and the government privatised it. The concessions that followed focused mainly on freight. These have been useful: trains play an important part in North America’s supply chains, hauling cars, grain, fuel and manufactured goods between ports, factories and the American border. But passenger services all but vanished.
paragraphs.zh: 墨西哥曾经有一张还算可用的客运铁路网络。然而到20世纪90年代，铁路系统已明显衰败，政府随后实施了私有化。此后推出的特许经营协议主要侧重货运。这一方向是有价值的：铁路在北美供应链中发挥着重要作用，在港口、工厂与美墨边境之间运输汽车、谷物、燃料和制造品。但客运服务几乎完全消失了。

paragraphs.en: Recent efforts to revive passenger trains have been fraught with controversy—in large part because they started with a dud. Tren Maya, a pet project of Ms Sheinbaum’s predecessor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, runs for 1,500km around the Yucatán peninsula in the south-east. It has caused huge environmental damage and its planning was influenced by political considerations. Stations were built in inconvenient locations without good connecting transport. “We have not seen the benefits we were told we would,” says a tour guide in Calakmul, one of the stations that bears the name of a nearby archaeological site.
paragraphs.zh: 最近复兴客运列车的努力充满争议，在很大程度上因为一开始就碰上了“坑” 。“特伦·玛雅”是前任总统安德烈斯·曼努埃尔·洛佩斯·奥夫拉多（Claudia Sheinbaum前任）的一项心头工程，全线约1,500公里，横跨东南部的尤卡坦半岛。它造成了巨大的环境破坏，且其规划明显受政治考量影响。站点建设在并不便利的地点，且缺乏良好的接驳交通。加拉克穆尔的一位导游——也是一个以附近考古遗址命名的车站——说：“我们没有看到我们被承诺会看到的那些好处。”

paragraphs.en: Ms Sheinbaum’s plans have more rigour. Andrés Lajous, the official in charge, says the idea is to connect contiguous metropolitan areas: from Mexico City to Pachuca and Querétaro, then on towards Irapuato and Guadalajara, and from Saltillo through Monterrey towards Nuevo Laredo (see map). These lines will connect cities that are slow to drive between but too close for flying to be sensible. Planned and built properly, they could save people time, cut pollution and spur economic growth. They could also stem urban sprawl, since rail makes it easier for people to live in one town while holding down jobs in another.
paragraphs.zh: 谢恩鲍姆女士的方案更为严谨。负责该项目的安德烈斯·拉霍斯称，设想是把相邻的都市圈连接起来：从墨西哥城到帕丘卡和克雷塔罗，再向伊拉普阿托和瓜达拉哈拉延伸；以及从萨尔蒂约经蒙特雷通往新拉雷多（见地图）。这些线路将连通那些驾车时间很长、却不适合乘机往返的城市。若规划和建设得当，它们可以节省民众出行时间、减少污染，并带动经济增长，也有助于遏制城市蔓延，因为铁路让人们可以在一个城镇居住、在另一城镇工作。

paragraphs.en: The idea is for the passenger lines to be built alongside existing freight ones, meaning the right of way is already mostly secured. (They will avoid running them on the same tracks, which can clog up the lines and limit speeds, as tends to happen in the United States.) The government is moving fast: it recently inaugurated a line to Felipe Ángeles airport, north of Mexico City, and has begun work on others to Pachuca and Querétaro.
paragraphs.zh: 该设想是客运线将与现有货运线并行建设，这意味着线路走廊大体上已有既定通道可以使用（也就是说，他们会避免让客运与货运共用同一条轨道，以免发生像美国常见那样的拥堵并限制速度）。政府推进得很快：它最近在墨西哥城以北的费利佩·安赫莱斯机场正式开通了一条线路，并已开始建设去帕丘卡和克雷塔罗的线路。

paragraphs.en: But to reach its destination the government will have to confront several challenges. One is whether there will really be enough cash. Passenger rail almost always requires public money: both to build it, and then to keep fares affordable. (Only a handful of high-density routes worldwide, such as Paris to Lyon and Tokyo to Osaka, are profitable.) Mexico has shallow coffers and many other needs. Oscar Ocampo of IMCO, a think-tank in Mexico City, reckons that only some of Ms Sheinbaum’s proposed lines make obvious sense (such as Mexico City to Querétaro, and Saltillo to Monterrey). The case for others is “not so clear”. Some of the money would be better spent further improving things such as freight rail, ports and industrial-corridor connections, he says.
paragraphs.zh: 但要把这些计划落实到位，政府必须面对多重挑战。其一，是否真的有足够的资金。客运铁路几乎总是需要公共财政支持：既要用于建设，也要用于后续保持票价可负担。世界上只有少数高客流量线路（例如巴黎至里昂、东京至大阪）能够盈利。墨西哥财政并不宽裕，且需求众多。墨西哥城智库IMCO的奥斯卡·坎波指出，只有谢恩鲍姆女士提出的部分线路从经济上才显得“显而易见”——比如墨西哥城到克雷塔罗，以及萨尔蒂约到蒙特雷；其他线路的合理性“不那么明确”。他认为，一部分资金若用于进一步改善货运铁路、港口和工业走廊连接等，会更为明智。

paragraphs.en: A second question is whether Mexico’s government intends to do far too much of the work itself. Mr López Obrador’s administration put the state—and the army—at the centre of infrastructure delivery, keeping private firms at arm’s length. Ms Sheinbaum has recognised that she must be less dogmatic. The state will lead the rail roll-out and operate the train services (not uncommon globally for big rail projects). The idea is that private companies will build substantial portions of the infrastructure. Big tenders are going out for stations, bridges, signalling, electrification and more. But keeping a correct balance may be difficult.
paragraphs.zh: 第二个问题是墨西哥政府是否打算把太多工作自己来做。洛佩斯·奥夫拉多尔总统任内的政府把国家和军队置于基础设施交付的核心，让私营企业保持距离。谢恩鲍姆意识到必须更务实一些。国家将主导铁路铺设并负责列车运营（对于大型铁路项目来说，这在世界范围并不罕见）。设想是让私人公司建设大量基础设施。大规模招标已经启动，内容包括车站、桥梁、信号系统、电气化等。但要把角色与资源的比例拿捏到位可能并不容易。

paragraphs.en: Last, Ms Sheinbaum will have to resist the temptation to prioritise speed over quality during development. Services along the Interoceanic railway—another of her predecessor’s projects, linking the port of Coatzacoalcos on the Gulf of Mexico with Salina Cruz on the Pacific coast—have been suspended since a train derailed in December, killing 14 people. Swift, efficient travel is worth investing in. So is arriving in one piece. ■
paragraphs.zh: 最后，谢恩鲍姆女士必须抵挡住在建设过程中优先追求速度而牺牲质量的诱惑。横跨洲际铁路——她前任的另一项工程，将墨西哥湾海岸的科阿查卡尔科斯港与太平洋海岸的萨利纳·克鲁斯相连——自去年12月一列火车脱轨、造成14人死亡以来已被暂停。快速高效的出行值得投资，完整安全地到达也同样重要。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to El Boletín, our subscriber-only newsletter on Latin America, to understand the forces shaping a fascinating and complex region.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《El Boletín》——我们面向订阅者的拉丁美洲通讯——以理解塑造这个迷人而复杂地区的各种力量。
